summary: McKenzie reports that his organic search traffic has surpassed his paid search (PPC) traffic for the first time. He discovered that his blog ranked higher than his main site for key search terms, so he added internal links from blog posts to product pages. Within 48 hours of being re-crawled, his site jumped from page 3 to page 1 of search results. He sees significant upside in capturing even a fraction of the organic search volume.
key_points: Organic search traffic exceeded PPC traffic for the first time, marking a milestone in the site's growth
Blog content inadvertently outranked the main product site, revealing the SEO power of regularly updated content
Strategic internal linking from blog to product pages produced rapid ranking improvements within 48 hours
He deliberately obfuscates his target keywords to avoid attracting competition to his niche
money_quotes: Today was the first day I got more organic hits than this, and they appear to be legitimate (i.e. not from pirates for a change).
I discovered that it ranks higher than my site for key search terms (as a result of a post similar to this one, ironically)
I made about 10 links for those terms from the blog to the appropriate content pages.
And, bam, 48 hours later I got crawled and my site went from the 3rd page of search results to the 1st page.
I know from my PPC search impressions that some of these words get traffic in the hundreds per day, which if I could capture 10% of would be excellent.
key_insight: A blog's higher domain authority can be leveraged through strategic internal linking to rapidly boost a product site's organic search rankings, reducing dependence on paid traffic.
